title: LSP: handle OccurrenceDef in analysis traversals
analysis: Four functions in the LSP crate only pattern-match on Declaration::Structure, ignoring Declaration::Occurrence. Since OccurrenceDef has identical fields to StructureDef (name, doc, is_pub, type_params, trait_bounds, members, span, content_hash), adding parallel match arms is straightforward. The affected functions are: (1) find_parsed_member_span_and_doc in analysis.rs — makes occurrence members invisible to hover and find_member_decl, (2) find_entity_doc in analysis.rs — occurrence doc comments unreachable, (3) structure_names in analysis.rs — occurrences missing from hover summaries and completions, (4) compute_goto_definition in goto_def.rs — goto-def fails on occurrence members. The structure_names function is special because its return value feeds into hover.rs (line 48) which hardcodes 'structure' in the display string; we add a kind discriminator to the return type so hover correctly shows 'occurrence' for occurrence declarations. The compiler already handles Declaration::Occurrence and member_names()/get_value() work off compiled templates, so those paths need no changes.

steps.description: Write failing test `find_member_decl_occurrence_param` in analysis.rs::tests — parse 'occurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm }', call find_member_decl("diameter"), assert returns Some with name="diameter", kind=Param. Currently fails because find_parsed_member_span_and_doc only matches Declaration::Structure.

Add Declaration::Occurrence(o) arm to find_parsed_member_span_and_doc in analysis.rs (line 88). Duplicate the Structure arm logic: iterate o.members, match Param/Let by name, return (span, doc). Step-1 test passes.

Write failing test `find_entity_doc_returns_doc_for_occurrence` in analysis.rs::tests — parse '/// A joint process.\noccurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm }', call find_entity_doc("Joint"), assert returns Some("A joint process."). Currently fails because find_entity_doc only matches Declaration::Structure.
Add Declaration::Occurrence(o) arm to find_entity_doc in analysis.rs (line 111). Match o.name == name, return o.doc.as_deref(). Step-3 test passes.

Write failing test `structure_names_includes_occurrence` in analysis.rs::tests — parse source with both 'structure Bracket { param width: Scalar = 80mm }' and 'occurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm\n  let radius = diameter / 2\n  constraint diameter > 5mm }', call structure_names(), assert result has 2 entries: Bracket with kind="structure" (1 param, 0 lets, 0 constraints) and Joint with kind="occurrence" (1 param, 1 let, 1 constraint). Fails because structure_names doesn't match Occurrence and doesn't return kind.

Change structure_names return type from Vec<(&str, usize, usize, usize)> to Vec<(&str, usize, usize, usize, &str)> adding kind as 5th element ("structure" or "occurrence"). Add Declaration::Occurrence(o) arm with identical counting logic. Update callers to compile: (a) hover.rs line 45 destructure adds 'kind' variable, (b) completion.rs line 53 destructure adds '_kind', (c) existing test structure_names_returns_bracket adds kind assertion. Do NOT yet use kind in hover format string (step-10). Steps 5 and existing tests pass.

Add Declaration::Occurrence(o) arm to compute_goto_definition in goto_def.rs (line 22). Duplicate the Structure arm: iterate o.members, match Param/Let by name, return Location with span. Step-7 test passes.

Write failing test `hover_on_occurrence_name_shows_occurrence_keyword` in hover.rs::tests — parse 'occurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm }', hover on 'Joint' (line 0, col 20), assert markdown contains 'occurrence Joint' (not 'structure Joint'). Fails because hover.rs hardcodes 'structure' in the format string at line 48.

Update hover.rs line 48 format string from hardcoded 'structure' to use the kind variable from structure_names() destructuring (already available from step-6). Change format!(..."structure {name}"...) to format!(..."{kind} {name}"...). Step-9 test passes.

Write verification test `hover_on_occurrence_member_shows_param_info` in hover.rs::tests — parse 'occurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm }', hover on 'diameter' (line 0 after occurrence keyword offset), assert markdown contains 'param' and 'diameter' and 'Scalar'. Should already pass (find_member_decl fixed in step-2, hover delegates to it).

Write verification test `completions_include_occurrence_names` in completion.rs::tests — parse 'occurrence def Joint { param diameter: Scalar = 10mm }', call compute_completions, assert items include 'Joint' with kind STRUCT. Should already pass (structure_names includes occurrences from step-6, completion.rs iterates it).

design_decisions.decision: Duplicate match arms rather than extracting a shared trait or helper function
design_decisions.rationale: StructureDef and OccurrenceDef are distinct types in the type system despite identical fields. Extracting a trait (e.g. EntityLike with name/doc/members accessors) would require changes to reify-syntax which is out of scope. Duplication is minimal (3-5 lines per site) and the task description explicitly suggests 'each match arm can be duplicated or extracted into a helper'.

design_decisions.decision: Add kind string as 5th tuple element to structure_names() return type rather than keeping return type unchanged
design_decisions.rationale: hover.rs hardcodes 'structure' in the display string at line 48. Without a kind discriminator, occurrences would display as 'structure Joint' which is incorrect. Adding kind='structure'|'occurrence' at the end of the tuple minimizes diff on existing destructuring patterns (callers just append one more binding).

design_decisions.decision: Keep the method named structure_names() rather than renaming to entity_names()
design_decisions.rationale: Renaming would be a larger refactor touching all callers and test names. The method now returns both structures and occurrences but its primary use case and name remain intuitive. A rename can be done in a future cleanup pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use CompletionItemKind::STRUCT for occurrence names in completions
design_decisions.rationale: LSP has no built-in 'occurrence' completion kind. STRUCT is the closest semantic match since occurrences are structurally identical to structures. No change needed in completion.rs beyond destructuring the new tuple element.

reuse.how: Existing tests use this for Structure testing; occurrence tests use inline source strings since bracket_source only contains a structure
All test modules define fn test_uri() -> Url { Url::parse("file:///test.ri").unwrap() }; hover.rs also has hover_markdown() helper that extracts markdown from hover result
Runs parse→compile→check pipeline; occurrence support flows through automatically since reify-compiler already handles Declaration::Occurrence
Each Occurrence arm is an exact duplicate of the adjacent Structure arm, accessing the same field names (name, doc, members, span)
